channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KREX 5

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UAE Supreme Court upholds sentences issued in mass trial of dissidents widely criticized abroad
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United Arab Emirates' Federal Supreme Court rejected appeals Tuesday on behalf of dozens of dissidents convicted in a mass trial that faced widespread international criticism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Investigators will question a driver who allegedly rammed a car into a crowd in Germany, killing 2
item.description: MANNHEIM, Germany (AP) — Investigators on Tuesday will question a driver who allegedly rammed a car into a crowd in the southwestern German city of Mannheim, killing two people and injuring 11.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and MICHAEL PROBST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pope Francis off mechanical ventilation, now on supplemental oxygen
item.description: Pope Francis had stabilized enough Tuesday after two respiratory crises to be taken off noninvasive mechanical ventilation and receive just high flows of supplemental oxygen
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: EU ponders 800 billion euro plan to beef up defenses to counter possible US disengagement
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The chief of the European Union's executive on Tuesday proposed an 800 billion-euro ($841 billion) plan to beef up the defenses of EU nations, aiming to lessen the impact of potential U.S.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New York prison guards fired for ignoring deal to end strike, thousands set to lose health insurance
item.description: New York officials have started to fire state prison guards who failed to comply with a deal to end their illegal labor strike, which is now in its third week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trans vegan cult member linked to Vermont border agent killing was person of interest in parents' murders
item.description: A double murder of a Pennsylvania couple in 2022 has been linked to the recent fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A first-term Michigan senator is set to deliver the Democratic response to Trump's speech
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — The Democratic response to President Donald Trump’s first joint congressional address of his second presidency will be delivered by a freshman Michigan senator who built an extensive national security background before entering politics less than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Orleans rushes to rework Mardi Gras celebrations in the face of storm and twister warnings
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Officials rushed to reschedule Mardi Gras celebrations and warned revelers against brining umbrellas and tents as powerful storms with a threat of tornadoes were expected to strike Louisiana and other parts of the South on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump will stand before Congress and offer divided nation an accounting of his turbulent first weeks
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Census Bureau under Trump seeks permission to delete questions about gender identity
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau under the Trump administration has sought permission to delete questions about gender identity from a monthly survey.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Orleans to celebrate Mardi Gras Day with costumes and revelry despite weather concerns
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Thousands of revelers adorned in beads and outlandish costumes will fill the streets of New Orleans as the city celebrates Mardi Gras Day despite anticipated severe storms.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How South Carolina's execution of a condemned killer by firing squad will unfold
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When a South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat steps into the death row chamber Friday night, it won’t be lethal injection or electrocution that ends his life.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Head of FBI New York office says he's retired from the bureau after being ordered to do so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of the FBI's New York field office, who was reported to have resisted Justice Department efforts to scrutinize agents who participated in politically sensitive investigations, has told coworkers that he has retired from the bureau after being directed to do so.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan mom seeks 'respect' for her 3 missing boys, asks a judge to declare them dead
item.description: ADRIAN, Mich. (AP) — The mother of three missing Michigan boys asked a judge Monday to declare them dead nearly 15 years later, saying they deserve the respect that they didn't get at the end of their young lives.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Slain California fire captain's fugitive wife 'scared' ex mother-in-law
item.description: The former mother-in-law of a woman accused of murdering her fire captain wife in Southern California said that she was "very scary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Black graves are being moved to make way for an industrial park, drawing a mix of emotions
item.description: DANVILLE, Va. (AP) — A decision to move the remains of hundreds African American tenant farmers from a former Virginia tobacco plantation to a dedicated burial ground has elicited a range of emotions among the sharecroppers’ descendants.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Killer whales amaze Seattle onlookers with a rarely seen bird hunt
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A pod of orcas swam close to shore and amazed onlookers in Seattle by treating the whale watchers to the rare sight of the apex predators hunting a bird.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cat food recalled over risk of bird flu weeks after pets euthanized
item.description: The Wild Coast LLC product 16oz and 24oz frozen Boneless Free Range Chicken Formula raw pet food for cats was distributed to pet food retailers in Washington State and Oregon. The product is frozen in a small round white plastic container with a green label.
item.dc:creator: Erin Yudt, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: JD Vance's role in Zelenskyy clash puts spotlight on VP
item.description: Vice President JD Vance's vocal involvement was among several noteworthy moments and led to mixed reactions.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: California Highway Patrol blames drug use, speeding for fiery Cybertruck crash that killed 3
item.description: PIEDMONT, Calif. (AP) — Drug use and excessive speed were to blame for last year's fiery crash of a Tesla Cybertruck that killed three college students near San Francisco, the California Highway Patrol said in a preliminary report.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chipotle will absorb tariff-related cost increases, CEO says
item.description: With the Trump administration's 25% tariff on all Mexican imports set to take effect Tuesday, Chipotle has pledged to absorb potential cost increases.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: “This is a one time offer in advance of a very significant Reduction in Force for the US Department of Education,” Jacqueline Clay said.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: The Education Department sent out an email to staffers on Friday saying if they quit by Monday at midnight, they will receive a $25,000 cash payout.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Cat food recalled due to bird flu risk
item.description: The FDA is alerting pet owners that some raw cat food is being recalled because it could be contaminated with bird flu.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Oscars 2025: Michelle Trachtenberg, Shannen Doherty, more left out of In Memoriam segment
item.description: As is nearly always the case, a few famous faces were missing from the In Memoriam segment — and their fans were quick to notice.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Kroger CEO resigns amid personal conduct investigation
item.description: McMullen's departure comes months after Kroger's failed two-year effort to merge with another supermarket giant, Albertsons.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: This rare bean may be your future coffee as warming climate hurts crops
item.description: As leading coffee-producing countries struggle to grow crops in drier, less reliable weather, prices have soared to the highest in decades and the industry is scrambling for solutions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: His announcement comes just after the White House announced it would be welcoming cryptocurrency industry professionals on March 7 in a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Inside Trump’s federal worker firings
item.description: Some of the removals have targeted a series of officials sure to conduct oversight of Trump’s actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Photos: The 2025 Oscars Red Carpet in Los Angeles
item.description: Hollywood's biggest stars and most talented artists are set to be honored Sunday evening during the 97th Academy Awards held at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood. But before the awards ceremony itself begins, the stars are walking the iconic Red Carpet where fashion takes center stage.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's next first speech to Congress is bound to have little resemblance to his last first one
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation will hear a new president sing a far different tune in his prime-time address before Congress on Tuesday night. Some Americans will lustily sing along. Others will plug their ears.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada man pleads guilty to extorting Instagram users, influencers
item.description: A Las Vegas man has pleaded guilty to extorting and threatening Instagram users and influencers, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5
